data.title: Shinsegae to Cancel More Than 200,000 Shares Annually Until 2027
Shinsegae to Cancel KRW35.36B of Shares on Feb. 27
Market Chatter: BP Reviews Options for Lubricants Business Including Sale
data.description:
BP (BP.L) is considering various options for its lubricants business Castrol, including a potential divestment, Bloomberg News reported Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources said the business could be worth $10 billion, adding that the sale is being considered to appease investors after years of underperformance, with a potential announcement of the plan expected during the British oil and gas company's capital markets day on Feb. 26.

The people added that activist investor Elliott Investment Management earmarked the business as one of the assets that BP could sell. Deliberations are ongoing and a final decision is yet to be made, the source noted.

BP did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Space42, Edge Ink AED378 Million Deal for UAE Earth Observation
data.description:
Space42's (ADX:SPACE42) Bayanat Smart Solutions and advanced technology group Edge's Fada entity signed a 378 million-Emirati-dirham contract to improve the United Arab Emirates' geospatial capabilities.

The five-year partnership will use the space technology company's Foresight Constellation of SAR satellite data and Fada's geospatial analytics to provide earth observation technologies and insights for various sectors, according to a Tuesday release.

data.title: Hexaware Technologies Shares at INR758.40 Vs. IPO Price of INR708
Hexaware Technologies IPO Raised INR87.5B, or About $1.01B
Australia's Central Bank Need Not Fret About Cutting Rates — Market Talk
data.description: Australia's wage growth continues to cool and the unemployment rate is hovering around the historic low of 4.0%. The data suggests that estimates of full employment held by central bank policy makers are incorrect, according to economists. The Reserve Bank of Australia delivered its first cut in official interest rates since 2020 on Tuesday, but appeared extremely hesitant when making the announcement, citing ongoing inflation risk. One reason for the concern is that the RBA puts full employment at around 4.5%. Madeline Dunk, economist at ANZ, says full employment is closer to 3.75%. If that's the case the RBA need not fret. (james.glnn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: US SEC seeks India's help in Adani fraud probe
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has asked Indian authorities for help in its investigation of Adani Group founder Gautam Adani and his nephew over alleged securities fraud and a $265-million bribery scheme, a court filing showed on Tuesday.

The regulator told a New York district court it was making efforts to serve its complaint on the founder and his nephew, Sagar Adani, and was seeking help from India's law ministry to do so.

Neither individual is in U.S. custody, and both are now in India.

"The SEC has requested assistance ... under the Hague service convention," it said in the court filing.

Adani Group and India's law ministry did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Last week, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said he did not discuss the Adani case with U.S. President Donald Trump during his visit to Washington, describing it to reporters as an individual issue never discussed by leaders.

India's opposition Congress party has called for Adani's arrest and accused Modi of shielding him or favouring him in deals in the past. Modi's party and Adani have denied the charges.

Last year, federal prosecutors in Brooklyn unsealed an indictment accusing Adani of bribing Indian officials to convince them to buy electricity produced by Adani Green Energy , a subsidiary of his Adani Group.

He then misled U.S. investors by providing reassuring information about the company's anti-graft practices, it added.

Adani Group has called the accusations "baseless" and vowed to seek "all possible legal recourse".

In January, Adani Green said it had appointed independent law firms to review the U.S. indictment.

data.title: Announcing the Resolutions of Board Meeting
data.description:
Title :

Announcing the Resolutions of Board Meeting NATIONAL DETERGENT

Content :

The board of directors discussed the topics listed on the agenda in its meeting no. 1 / 22 held on 18/02/2025 and passed the following resolutions.

Decision of Board of Directors meeting held on 18th February 2025

The Board of Directors of M/s The National Detergent Co SAOG has taken the following decisions in their meeting held on 18th February 2025.

1. The Board has approved the Financial Statements for the year ended 31st December 2024 in which the company achieved net profit after tax of RO 1,393,616 (previous year a profit of RO 657,519). These financial statements are subject to the approval of the Shareholders at the Annual General Meeting to be held on 23rd March 2025.

2. The Board has proposed a cash dividend of 50 Bz per share for the year ended 31st December 2024 subject to the shareholders' approval at the Annual General Meeting to be held on 23rd March 2025.

For The National Detergent Co SAOG

Murali Sundar Kothandaraman Arun

data.title: SportsHero Says Indosat Opts for Full Commercial Deployment of Cloud-Based Gaming Platform; Shares Hit 52-Week High
data.description:
SportsHero said Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison opted to proceed directly to a full commercial deployment of the iGV.Com Family Game Room cloud-based gaming platform, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The platform is expected to launch in the June quarter.

Both companies will expedite technology integration.

Skipping the proof-of-concept phase will accelerate integration and implementation to bring forward product launch and potential revenue generation for SportsHero and Indosat, per the filing.

Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison is a subsidiary of Ooredoo Hutchison Asia.

The firm's shares rose past 7% in recent trading on Wednesday, hitting a 52-week high.

data.title: JES International to Delist on Feb. 26
data.description:
JES International will be delisted from the Singapore Stock Exchange on Feb. 26 at 9:00 am, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Tuesday.

In an earlier filing, the company had concluded that it does not have the financial resources to make an exit offer compliant with the rules of the listing manual.

data.title: ForexLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: RBNZ’s 3rd consecutive 50bp rate cut, NZD higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P ratings revise Australian state Queensland rating outlook to negative
  • Would-be Kishida assassin gets 10 years in jail
  • Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson is speaking after market close on Wednesday
  • Goldman Sachs says OPEC+ will delay its gradual production increases from April to July
  • ANZ forecast 3 25bp rate cuts from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand, in April May and July
  • More on Trump's proposed 25% tariff on automobiles, pharmaceuticals & semiconductor chips
  • RBNZ Governor Orr says NZD around fair value
  • J.P. Morgan upgrades Singapore equities to overweight on strong outlook, target 6% gain
  • More from RBNZ Governor Orr, says 3.75% is the high end of the range of neutral rates
  • RBNZ Governor Orr confirms a rate cut in April and then May sounds about right
  • China’s new home prices remain stagnant in January amid property market struggles
  • BOJ's Takata says must hike rate more if economy moves in line with BOJ forecasts
  • Reserve Bank of New Zealand rate cut news conference begins 0200 GMT/2100 US Eastern time
  • New Bank of Japan board Junko Koeda approved by Japan's parliament
  • NZD/USD lower after the as expected 50bp rate cut from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand
  • PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 7.1705 (vs. estimate at 7.2807)
  • RBNZ cuts cash rate by 50bp vs. 50bp expected
  • Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley shifted its BOJ rate hike call to July, from Q4 previously
  • Bank of Japan Board Member Takata Hajime is speaking soon
  • Australian data - Wage Price Index (Q4 2024 ) +0.7% q/q (expected +0.8%)
  • Japan data - January exports and imports improve y/y, December machine orders improve y/y
  • Reserve Bank of New Zealand expected to cut official cash rate (OCR) target by 50bp today,
  • Japan manufacturing sentiment improves second consecutive month, services sector stable
  • Bank of America Survey: Trade war fears rise as US equities remain overvalued
  • JP Morgan on Trump’s social media activity showing diminished market-moving influence
  • Trump said he may not let Venezuela export oil, petroleum products, via firms like Chevron
  • Bitcoin's Path to $500K: Institutional interest grows amid (trimmed) regulatory hopes
  • New Zealand Q4 PPI falls q/q
  • Trump says "will be announcing" large firms that are coming back to the US re Chips, cars
  • Trade ideas thread - Wednesday, 19 February, insightful charts, technical analysis, ideas
  • Forexlive Americas FX news wrap: US dollar firms and US stocks close at a record

In late afternoon news from the US, Trump signalled he could slap a 25% tariff on imported automobiles, especially targeting those from Europe and Asia (China, Japan and South Korea are notable large exporters of vehicles). A similar tariff could also hit pharmaceuticals and semiconductor chips. Trump indicated these could apply as soon as April 2.

Nothing is solid on this as yet, but it does raise further trade war implication concerns.

Trump also said he may not let Venezuela export oil and petroleum products via firms like Chevron.

The main focus for the session was the Reserve Bank of New Zealand. The Bank cut its cash rate target by 50bp, the third 50bp rate cut in a row. Guidance was dovish, the OCR path indicating a further cut by 50bps by mid-this year. This is expected to be two 25bp cuts although ANZ in New Zealand are expecting three 25bp cuts, one each in April, May and July (the next three meetings, the 9th, 28th and 9th respectively).

The New Zealand dollar chopped up and then down after the announcement, settling around lows circa 0.5680 for a half hour or so before surging back to record a new high for the session above 0.5720. There is more on the RBNZ in the points above.

AUD/USD followed the NZD higher.

From Japan we had comments from Bank of Japan policy board member Takata. He said (in brief) that Japan's real interest rates remain deeply negative and are still accommodative, but:

  • the Bank must adjust degree of monetary support further if economy moves in line with its forecasts,
  • the Bank must gradually shift policy to avoid upside price risks from materialising.

USD/JPY is not really net changed much at all on the day, but it did pop briefly to just over 152.30 before coming back to lows circa 151.80.

From China we had January home price data. New home prices recorded their 20th straight m/m % drop. 2nd hand home prices hit 21 straight falls.

Silver fell circa 1%.

data.title: BOJ's Takata Calls for Gradual Rate Hikes, Sees Upside Risks to Prices — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--The Bank of Japan needs to keep adjusting the degree of monetary accommodation, policy board member Hajime Takata said Wednesday, as he warned about the risk of inflation climbing too high.

Takata also expressed optimism about the outlook for the U.S. economy despite growing worries about the impact of President Trump's policies, saying strength in the U.S. could buoy Japan's economy too.

"It is important to shift gears gradually--even after the additional interest-rate hike in January--to avoid the risks of price upswings and financial overheating from becoming a reality," Takata said in a speech to business leaders in Japan's northern prefecture of Miyagi.

Takata said that further rate hikes would be possible if corporate activity--including the passing on of costs to consumers, wage increases and capital spending--continues, and if the bank's economic outlook is progressively realized.

The Japanese central bank raised the policy rate to 0.5% in late January and has pledged to seek more interest-rate hikes if the economy and prices develop in line with its forecasts.

Many economists expect the BOJ's next tightening move to come around the summer time so that it can sustain the pace of one hike in every six months. In a reflection of those hopes, the yen has stopped weakening against the dollar, and Japanese government bond yields have reached multi-year highs.

Turning to the issue of U.S. policy risks, Takata, a veteran market economist before joining the BOJ, said that improvement in the U.S. economy would brighten the outlook for Japan.

Though attention needs to be paid to uncertainties around the new U.S. administration's policymaking, Takata said he believes the possibility of a re-acceleration in the U.S. economy, rather than a soft landing, is rising.

"If overseas economies, particularly the U.S., perform better, the momentum of [Japan's economic] recovery may strengthen," Takata said. Comfort over the outlook for the world's largest economy could help reduce market volatility, the board member said, giving the Japanese central bank more flexibility in its policy-making.

That said, if strength in the U.S. economy causes the yen to weaken and Japanese companies give big pay increases this year, prices could pick up faster than expected in Japan, he added.

His view contrasts with growing concerns about the negative economic impact of Trump's tariff threats. The U.S. president on Tuesday said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, a move that would likely have a significant impact on Japan, given that a sizable amount of its exports to the U.S. are auto-related.

Data earlier showed that Japan's exports grew for a fourth consecutive month in January but trade protectionism could hinder what has been a key engine of growth for the economy. Shipments to the U.S. rose 8.1% on the year in the month, in part due to solid demand for cars.

The U.S. was the standout destination for Japan's exports, but the jump may be "the calm before the Trumpian storm," ING economist Min Joo Kang said in a note.

Tariffs on auto exports would be particularly damaging for Japan's economy as the sector accounts for 15% of industrial production and spillover effects are remarkably high due to the intertwined nature of the domestic supply chain, Oxford Economics said in a recent report.

The U.S. also has the upper hand in potentially using tariffs as a means to gain diplomatic leverage, as shipments to the U.S. account for 20% of Japan's exports while Japan only makes up 5% of U.S. imports, said economist Makoto Tsuchiya.

"Even in the absence of bilateral tariffs on Japan, globalized supply chains mean Japan could still be hit by tariffs on other economies," he said.

The Japanese government has been closely communicating with the U.S. administration on tariffs. Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba said Monday that Japan differs from other countries because it is making a lot of investments in the U.S., creating jobs and contributing to wage increases for American workers.

"We are not asking for special treatment for Japan, but we will continue to demonstrate the legitimacy of our claims," Ishiba said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: New Zealand Shares Extend Muted Session After Rate Cut; Fletcher Building Jumps 5% as Loss Narrows in Fiscal H1
data.description:
New Zealand shares extended their muted session on Wednesday even as the Reserve Bank delivered a widely expected rate cut and as investors digested a string of corporate earnings reports.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index was little changed at 13,033.36.

Non-energy minerals led the stocks that rose, while miscellaneous equities were the biggest losers.

Asia-Pacific shares were mixed, with the Shanghai SSE up 0.5%, while the Nikkei 225 and Hang Seng lost 0.5% and 0.3%, respectively.

Overnight, the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to close at an all-time high, while the Nasdaq Composite and the Dow Jones Industrial Average were little changed.

In domestic news, the Reserve Bank of New Zealand lowered the official cash rate by 50 basis points to 3.75%, marking its fourth straight rate cut. In announcing the widely expected move, the bank said it has scope to lower the rate further through 2025 "if economic conditions continue to evolve as projected."

In corporate news, Fletcher Building climbed 5% after it reported Wednesday that its loss per diluted share in the fiscal first half slightly narrowed to NZ$0.143 from NZ$0.147 a year earlier.

EBOS Group lost 5% after it reported fiscal half-year diluted earnings of AU$0.569 per share, down from AU$0.71 per share a year earlier.

Ventia Services Group said its earnings per diluted share for 2024 rose to AU$0.2548, from AU$0.2201 per diluted share a year earlier.

data.title: Tesla picks showroom sites in New Delhi, Mumbai to start India sales, sources say
data.description:
Tesla has selected locations for two showrooms in the Indian cities of New Delhi and Mumbai, moving closer to its long-delayed plans to sell its electric cars in the country, sources familiar with the matter said.

The U.S. carmaker has been hunting for showroom space in India since late last year in order to start sales in the world's third biggest auto market, after putting market entry plans on hold in 2022.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Tesla CEO Elon Musk in the United States last week and discussed issues including space, mobility and technology.

Tesla has selected space to lease in the Aerocity area near New Delhi's international airport for a showroom, said two people familiar with the discussions, who declined to be named as the matter is private.

The Aerocity area boasts hotels, retail outlets and offices of global corporations.

In Mumbai, the carmaker has chosen space in the business and retail hub of Bandra Kurla Complex near the city's airport, the first person said. Both the Delhi and Mumbai showrooms will be around 5,000 square feet (464.52 square metres) in size, the source said.

Opening dates for the outlets have not yet been decided, but Tesla plans "to sell imported EVs in India ... these deals are for showrooms, not service centres," the first person said, adding that Tesla will operate the outlets.

Tesla did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The company also posted job ads this week for 13 mid-level roles in India, including some store and customer relationship managers.

Musk has long criticised India for having high import tariffs of around 100% on EVs. Tesla has repeatedly lobbied to relax them but has faced opposition from local automakers who think Tesla's entry could hit their EV plans.

U.S. President Donald Trump last week called out India's high duty on cars, but agreed with Modi to work towards an early trade deal and resolve their standoff over tariffs.

data.title: ZICO Flags Significant Net Loss for 2024
data.description:
ZICO Holdings expects to book a significant net loss for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Tuesday.

The expected losses are due to slower corporate finance, consultancy, and asset management activities amid a softer market, as well as impairment and write-off of certain trade receivables.

The company will release its financial results on or before Feb. 28.

data.title: China stocks rise on AI optimism; HK shares dip on profit-taking
data.description:
China shares rose on Wednesday, buoyed by gains in artificial intelligence (AI)-related firms that continued to lift market sentiment, while Hong Kong equities slipped as investors booked profits on some tech stocks.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index climbed 0.4% by the lunch break, while the Shanghai Composite Index gained 0.5%. Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng fell 0.3%.

** Chinese tech stocks (.CSIASITI) and AI-related shares (.CSI930713) led gains onshore, up 1.7% and 1.6%, respectively.

** The case for buying Chinese stocks, particularly tech shares, is improving, perhaps enough to tempt back long-term investors, investment banks said, noting the emergence of AI startup DeepSeek and a meeting between Xi Jinping and business leaders in the sector.

** The Hang Seng Index has surged 14% year-to-date, while the tech index has soared 27%, positioning the Hong Kong market as one of the best performers this year.

** Given the recent surge, selling at the open wouldn't be unexpected, said Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone. However, sentiment towards China's asset markets is shifting positively, justifying the rally, with any pullbacks likely to be minor, Weston added.

** Alibaba and Baidu shed 1.2% and 2.8%, respectively.

** Meanwhile, chip stocks saw a surge, with China's largest chipmaker SMIC jumping more than 6% to a record high and Hua Hong Semiconductor soaring nearly 18%.

** China's new home prices stalled month-on-month in January, official data showed on Wednesday. Onshore property shares gained 0.5%.

data.title: Hong Kong's Shares Mixed; Chip Stocks Surge — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's shares are mixed with the benchmark Hang Seng Index paring earlier losses to be down 0.3% at midday and the Hang Seng Tech Index erasing opening losses to rise 0.4%. Chip stocks are leading the gains, with Hua Hong Semiconductor up 18% and SMIC 6.4% higher. Autos stock are extending their gains with Xpeng up 5.4%, Li Auto rising 3.7% and BYD Electronic 1.6% higher. There could be less upside for tech stocks after the recent run of rallies, say Citi Research analysts in a note. In the absence of macro fundamentals, there aren't many factors that can broaden and sustain this rally, and any escalation in U.S. trade disputes, or a lack of economic support policy, will be key downside risks, they add. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0439 GMT - Hong Kong's shares are mixed with the benchmark Hang Seng Index paring earlier losses to be down 0.3% at midday and the Hang Seng Tech Index erasing opening losses to rise 0.4%. Chip stocks are leading the gains, with Hua Hong Semiconductor up 18% and SMIC 6.4% higher. Autos stock are extending their gains with Xpeng up 5.4%, Li Auto rising 3.7% and BYD Electronic 1.6% higher. There could be less upside for tech stocks after the recent run of rallies, say Citi Research analysts in a note. In the absence of macro fundamentals, there aren't many factors that can broaden and sustain this rally, and any escalation in U.S. trade disputes, or a lack of economic support policy, will be key downside risks, they add. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0422 GMT - Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M)'s long-term growth may remain intact, despite short-term earnings facing pressure from warehouse issues and declining sales, CGS International analysts Lew Cheng Wei and Prem Jearajasingam say in a note. Concerns over weak 4Q 2024 results have driven the company's share price down 20% year-to-date, they note. However, store expansions, stable margins, improved consumer sentiment, and new retail brand KKV's contributions should support long-term growth. Positive macroeconomic factors, including wage hikes should boost consumer spending and sales growth in 2025, they add. CGS International maintains an add rating on Mr. D.I.Y. Group and keeps target price at MYR2.60. Shares are 2.0% lower at MYR1.47.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0418 GMT - New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.1% lower at 13033.36, falling for a second consecutive session of narrow declines, as the country's central bank slashed the cash rate by another 50 basis points. The benchmark index gave back earlier gains as the Reserve Bank of New Zealand also warned of rising potential for international trade tensions, slower global growth and geopolitical shocks. With the cash rate at 3.75%, the RBNZ has cut by 175 bps since mid-2024. Property and aged-care stocks were mixed, while consumer-related shares were weak. Retailer Hallenstein Glasson shed 0.9% and casino operator SkyCity fell 2.0%. Pharmaceutical supplier Ebos was the worst performer, falling 4.9% after announcing the retirement of its CEO. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - J&T Global Express's Southeast Asian business might not grow as fast as the Chinese delivery company expected, Morningstar's Chelsey Tam says in a commentary. The analyst expects the company's Southeast Asia express volume share to increase to 29% by 2028. That compares with J&T Express's goal of quickly increasing its volume market share in Southeast Asia to 40%, she notes. Increasing gross merchandise volume share gains for e-commerce platform Shopee and express volume share gains for its logistics arm, SPX Express, will limit J&T's upside volume share gain potential, Tam says. She keeps a fair-value estimate of HK$6.10 on J&T Express. Share are last at HK$6.54. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - Chinese equities will likely underperform this year despite the recent rally, says Capital Economics senior markets economist James Reilly. Chinese equities have outperformed in recent weeks amid the enthusiasm over artificial intelligence, he says in a note. While the outlook for Chinese equities has improved, the lack of checks and balances in China means that this attitude shift in the tech sector could reverse at a moment's notice, he says. The threat is always likely to weigh on valuations, he adds. Capital Economics remains doubtful that the benefits of AI will diffuse through China's economy. This means that AI-related gains may remain largely concentrated in the tech sector instead of pushing stronger earnings growth across other sectors in China's market.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0359 GMT - Complementary Metal Oxide Semiconductor image sensors are likely to attract more attention from investors in 2025, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. Drivers include likely sustained recovery in global smartphone demand, the analysts say. Another driver is ongoing car smartification trend which will boost average selling prices of automobiles' CMOS image sensors and the number of such sensors per car, they add. Sony Group and Will Semiconductor are key beneficiaries. The brokerage raises target price on Will Semiconductor's shares to CNY180.00 from CNY165.00 with unchanged buy rating. It has an outperform rating and target price of Y3,700 on Sony Group's shares. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0354 GMT - India's Sensex falls 0.4% to 75675.31. Banks are mixed, with ICICI Bank and HDFC Bank falling 0.7% and 0.4%, respectively. Kotak Mahindra Bank adds 0.3% and State Bank of India edges 0.2% higher. Sun Pharmaceutical Industries loses 2.6% and Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone falls 1.7%. Markets will be keeping an eye on U.S. FOMC meeting minutes and U.S. housing data due later today, UOB Global Economics & Markets Research writes in a note. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0339 GMT - Chinese president Xi's meeting with private business leaders is likely to boost confidence amid weak domestic demand and ongoing tariff disputes with the U.S., according to BofA Securities analysts in a note. The meeting, which was the first such symposium since 2018, was much needed given the fragile confidence among businesses and households, weak demand, as well as Trump's 10% additional tariffs on Chinese products, they note. The meeting highlighted a strong focus of technology, as five out of the six entrepreneurs who spoke were heads of high-tech businesses, they note. Xi's speech emphasizes the urgency of boosting business confidence and spurring technology breakthroughs as the U.S.-China technology rivalry intensifies, they add.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0331 GMT - The long-term outlook for Chinese equities may become more positive amid signs that major overhangs on the market are easing, Daiwa analysts write in a note. For years, the Chinese property downturn, Beijing's crackdown on the private sector and U.S. tech restrictions were the top three concerns for global investors interested in China, they say. But these overhangs have eased or even lifted over the past six months, Daiwa adds. The recent symposium between Chinese President Xi Jinping and private enterprise leaders should act as a stabilizer for the recent market boom, the analysts say. They remain cautious, however, noting the potential for a market pullback after China's Two Sessions meeting in March. The valuation gap between Chinese tech stocks and their U.S. peers has almost disappeared following China's weekslong market rally, the analysts add. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0330 GMT - China's property sector has shown early signs of a stabilization path, Citi analysts write in a note. Citi turns less bearish on the sector from February, citing policy expectations for the March NPC meeting as well as stabilizing sales from 4Q and the first two months of this year. Moreover, recent measures reportedly being considered to help China Vanke are positive signs signaling likely explicit government support, they note. Recent policies are moving in the right direction, but it may take time to translate into results, Citi analysts add. Among Chinese property developers, Citi prefers Ke Holdings, C&D International Investment Group, Greentown China and China Resources Land. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0324 GMT - Chinese stocks could be rerated amid improving fundamentals and a better operating environment in China, Deutsche Bank analyst Jessie Xu says in a research note. Chinese President Xi's meeting with tech leaders signals Beijing's refocussing on the role the private sector plays in innovation and driving growth, which would later benefit employment, household income and consumption, the analyst says. Meanwhile, even after the recent rally, Chinese tech leaders' stocks are still trading at a 60% discount to "the Magnificent 7," Xu notes. China stocks were severely discounted by the market, mainly due to concerns about regulatory risks. DB thinks the regulation has "hit rock bottom and reversed" and will unlikely flip again anytime soon. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0323 GMT - The AI investment theme that has buoyed Chinese equities still has legs, but risks related to U.S.-China tariff disputes remain on the horizon, OCBC analysts write in a note. With the U.S. due to conclude a tariff review across a range of countries by April 1, the potential for an escalation in trade disputes is highly likely to persist in 2Q, they say. The outcome of Trump's potential visit to China is another key event to keep an eye on, OCBC says. Domestically, the National People's Congress meeting in March should shed light on China's next steps in policy support, especially more clarity on quantifying fiscal stimulus, it adds. The brokerage reiterates its preference for leading internet and platform companies with exposure to the broadening and acceleration of AI adoption. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Basketball-Former Australia captain welcomes Irving's bid to switch allegiance
data.description:
Kyrie Irving's bid to switch allegiance to Australia has been welcomed by former Boomers captain Andrew Gaze, even if the chances of basketball authorities approving it appear slim.

Dallas Mavericks point guard Irving, who was born in Melbourne but represented the United States at the 2016 Rio Olympics, said this week he was in the process of making the switch and finding out the best route to become eligible.

Gaze said the 32-year-old would be a welcome addition to a Boomers squad that could boast NBA players Dyson Daniels and Josh Giddey at the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

"The combination is mouth-watering and one that we are all getting very excited about," five-times Olympian Gaze told Australian broadcaster Nine Network.

"Why wouldn't you be (excited)? He's a superstar of the NBA and has been for many years.

"Hopefully he can hang onto that for a few more years (and) pull on a green and gold jersey, and we as a country can get a gold medal around our necks."

Irving considered playing for Australia at the 2012 London Olympics but decided to focus on earning a spot on the Team USA roster for 2016.

The switch would need to be approved by global governing body FIBA along with Australia and the United States.

FIBA and USA Basketball did not provide immediate comment when contacted by Reuters on Wednesday.

Basketball Australia declined to comment.

FIBA bans players from representing more than one nation unless there are "exceptional circumstances".

A few players have successfully switched allegiance, including Vassil Evtimov, who represented his birth nation Bulgaria after playing for France.

FIBA also approved Anton Gavel's switch to Germany after representing Slovakia.

Australia were knocked out of the quarter-finals at the Paris Olympics after taking bronze at the Tokyo Games.

Gaze, who is second in all-time points scoring at the Olympics behind Brazilian Oscar Schmidt, said any player of Irving's talent would be welcome.

"Hopefully this time around with the level of motivation he seems to have right now, we can convince those at FIBA to get it done."

data.title: HSBC Posts Quarterly Net Profit, Plans $2.0 Billion Share Buyback
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

HSBC Holdings reported a quarterly net profit, supported by higher fee income, and said it plans to start a share buyback of up to $2.0 billion.

The London-based bank said Wednesday that it booked net profit of $197 million for the three months ended December, compared with a net loss of $153 million a year ago. That beat the estimated net profit of $27.9 million in a poll of analysts by Visible Alpha.

The bank's bottom line took a hit in the year-earlier period as it wrote down the value of its stake in major Chinese lender Bank of Communications by $3 billion.

Net interest income--the difference between interest earned on loans and that paid on deposits-- fell to $8.185 billion from $8.28 billion a year earlier, while net fee income climbed to $2.98 billion from $2.76 billion.

Chief Executive Georges Elhedery has been taking steps to simplify the bank's structure after taking the top job in early September.

Under his leadership, the bank merged its commercial- and investment-banking operations, cut the size of its executive committee by a third and plans to stop advising companies on doing deals and going public in the U.S. and Europe.

Elhedery is pushing the bank to focus on its strengths: retail banking in the U.K. and Hong Kong, acting as a bridge for large companies to global markets for their goods and services, and helping wealthy clients with their finances.

He wants to cut costs and speed up decision-making by trimming jobs across HSBC's 200,000-plus staff and criss-crossing layers of management.

Because of its role in straddling Western and Chinese markets, the lender is potentially exposed to trade and geopolitical tensions between the Trump administration and Beijing.

HSBC's shares in Hong Kong have risen 15% so far this year through Tuesday, following a 23% gain in 2024, partly driven by hopes for restructuring efforts under the new management led by Elhedery.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Indian pharma stocks drop on Trump's tariff plans
data.description:
Shares of Indian pharmaceutical companies slid 2% on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump said he intends to impose tariffs of about 25% or higher on pharma imports.

Seventeen of the 20 stocks on the sub-index traded lower, with Sun Pharma and Dr Reddy's falling 2% and 4%, respectively. Aurobindo Pharma tumbled 6%.

Indian drugmakers earn a significant share of revenue from the U.S., the largest importer of Indian pharma products.

India's pharma exports to the country stood at $8.73 billion in fiscal 2024, accounting for nearly 31% of the industry's overall exports, as per data from government-backed trade body Pharmaceuticals Export Promotion Council of India (Pharmexcil).

Trump on Tuesday told reporters that sectoral tariffs on pharmaceuticals and semiconductor chips would begin at "25% or higher", rising substantially over the course of a year, but did not provide a date for the announcement of those duties.

Trump added that he wanted to provide some time for drug and chip makers to set up U.S. factories so they could avoid tariffs.

Pharma stocks have lost about 12% so far this year, while the benchmark Nifty 50 declined 4%.

data.title: India's Hexaware Technologies rises 5% in market debut
data.description: Shares of Hexaware Technologies (HEXW.NS) rose 5% in their market debut on Wednesday, after its $1 billion initial public offering which saw institutional buyers rush in as retail investors held back.
